text:
University College Hospital 

National Hospital for Neurology and Neurosurgery 

Clinic Date: 13 August 2016 

Dr Helen Carter
Riverside Medical Practice
14 Brook Lane, London SE15 3QD

Dear Dr Helen Carter,

Michael Turner, DOB: 22-07-1974, Hospital No: U284731 NHS No. 6048291753
52 Alder Grove, London, N19 4PA

Summary: Appointment rearrangement due to temporary equipment maintenance. Past variability in community pharmacy supply noted with occasional impact on adherence.

Medication: Current antiepileptic regimen unchanged.

I am writing to reschedule the planned diagnostic test for Michael Turner. Our neurophysiology equipment is undergoing scheduled maintenance this week, and we are unable to perform the study on the original date.

New appointment details:
- Location: National Hospital for Neurology and Neurosurgery, Neurophysiology Unit
- Date/Time: To be confirmed within 7 days; our booking team will contact the patient directly

Plan:
- Rearrange test at the earliest available slot.
- Please advise the patient to continue regular medication without interruption.
- Given the history of variable pharmacy supply, kindly ensure prescription continuity and consider issuing an interim supply if needed prior to the new date.

We apologise for any inconvenience and appreciate your assistance in maintaining medication continuity.

Yours sincerely

check__Seizure Frequency Number.analysis: The letter discusses rescheduling a diagnostic test and maintaining antiepileptic medication continuity due to equipment maintenance and prior pharmacy supply variability. It does not mention seizures, seizure events, or any explicit or implicit seizure frequency, nor seizure-free duration.
reference: Create a reasonable NHS letter rescheduling an epilepsy-related test due to equipment maintenance

flaws.description: No seizure frequency referenced in gold
Row marked as not OK in raw dataset
